task: Implement a real-time chat feature with end-to-end-encrypted (E2EE) 1-on-1 message history over Socket.IO in the existing Split-Nest NestJS + MongoDB project, extensible to group chat.
target_model: Claude (Claude Code, run inside the Split-Nest repo)

project.name: Split-Nest
project.type: NestJS REST API for expense splitting

absolute_rules: Backend only. No client / React Native / UI code.
Match the existing project precisely: auth model, response envelope, folder layout, validation, naming. No parallel patterns.
The server never sees plaintext. It stores and routes ciphertext only, never decrypts, and never holds a private key.
Report the plan after Step 1 and wait for user confirmation before implementing the bulk.

folder_layout.schemas: Mongoose schemas live in src/schemas/, NOT in the feature folder. Put new schemas there.
folder_layout.module_registration: Register the new module in src/v1/v1.module.ts; import AuthModule (exports JwtAuthGuard).
folder_layout.types_folder: No types/ folder exists. Colocate interfaces with the feature.

response_envelope.http: Global ResponseInterceptor wraps every HTTP response as { success, statusCode, message, errors, data }; HttpExceptionFilter shapes errors (422 = validation, 409 = conflict).
response_envelope.controllers_rule: REST controllers return only the data payload. Never wrap responses manually.
response_envelope.websocket: WebSocket events do NOT pass through the HTTP interceptor/filter. Define one consistent emit payload shape and use it for every socket emit.

auth.model: Session-array based, not stateless JWT. User has sessions: [{ token, tokenExpiry }] (multi-device).
auth.guard_flow: JwtAuthGuard reads token from 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' or 'x-auth-token' -> verifies JWT -> finds matching session in user.sessions -> checks tokenExpiry -> sets request.user = { id, userName, emailId, token }.
auth.gateway_requirement: Socket.IO handleConnection MUST replicate this session-array verification, not just jwtService.verify. A valid signature is not enough; the token must still exist in user.sessions and be unexpired.
auth.shared_logic: Extract the guard's verification into a reusable AuthService method so the guard and gateway share one implementation. Do not duplicate it.

request_and_pagination.style: POST with a request body even for list/read operations.
request_and_pagination.default_pagination: Offset pagination via { pageNumber, itemNumber } in the body.
request_and_pagination.validation: class-validator DTOs in the feature's dto/ folder.

id_convention.note: Mixed in the codebase. GroupMember stores ObjectId-hex strings (memberID, groupID). Expense uses real ObjectId refs (userId -> ref User). Querying User by a string id uses new Types.ObjectId(id).
id_convention.decision_for_new_schemas: Follow the Expense pattern (real ObjectId refs) for the new chat schemas. State this in the plan.

verify: JwtAuthGuard internals (src/v1/auth/jwt-auth.guard.ts) and how AuthService loads the user + matches the session; pick the cleanest method to extract for reuse.
The User schema (src/schemas/user.schema.ts) to add the publicKey field.
How V1Module and existing modules import AuthModule.
step1_verify_then_plan.output: A findings summary + proposed folder structure + schema/endpoint/event plan.
step1_verify_then_plan.then: Stop and wait for user confirmation before implementing.

target_structure.note: Public-key endpoints may live in the existing AuthModule instead (user/auth-related). Pick what fits and state it.

user_edit.note: base64 Curve25519 public key. Safe to store. Must not affect existing auth flows.

fields.groupID: reference the existing Group for group conversations (reuse Group/GroupMember for membership, do not duplicate a members list)

message.no_plaintext: No plaintext 'content' field. E2EE payload only.
message.index: compound { conversationId: 1, createdAt: -1 } for fast paginated history

group_chat_reuse: For type 'group' conversations, derive join/send rights from the existing GroupMember membership of the linked groupID. Reuse the existing role/permission model. Do not build a second membership system.

gateway.handleConnection: Read handshake.auth.token, run the shared session-verification (verify JWT + match user.sessions + check expiry). On failure client.disconnect(). On success set client.data.user and register the socket in presence.service.
gateway.handleDisconnect: Remove the socket; user is offline only when their socket set is empty (multi-device, consistent with sessions model).
gateway.scaling_note: Keep the gateway free of any assumption that a socket lives in the local process so a Socket.IO Redis adapter can be added later. Do NOT implement Redis now.

events.name: join-room
events.behavior: Verify the user is a participant (direct) or GroupMember (group), then join room = conversationId.

events.name: send-message
events.behavior: Validate DTO, persist ciphertext via chat.service, then emit 'new-message' to the room. Never decrypt.
events.payload: { conversationId, type, encryptedForReceiver, encryptedForSender, nonce, encryptionVersion }

events.name: new-message
events.behavior: Live delivery of a saved (ciphertext) message to the room.

events.name: typing
events.behavior: Broadcast 'user-typing' to the room excluding the sender. Not persisted.

events.name: read-receipt
events.behavior: Update readBy, emit 'message-read' to the room.

websocket.events_file: Define event-name constants + payload interfaces in chat.events.ts. Use one consistent emit shape.

rest_endpoints.description: Fetch a user's public key (POST-body to match project style).
Create a direct or group conversation.
Paginated list of the user's conversations (offset pagination).

Paginated message history, ciphertext only, newest-first. Use cursor pagination (beforeMessageID), NOT pageNumber offset, because offset duplicates/skips rows with live inserts. Keep POST-body style. Authorize: participant (direct) or GroupMember (group). Server never decrypts.

design_constraints: REST history is the source of truth; the socket is live delivery only. On reconnect the client backfills history via the cursor, so the history endpoint must support that.
Every endpoint/event requires the authenticated user (existing guard for REST, shared verification for WS).
Do not log ciphertext or tokens.
Do not manually wrap REST responses (the interceptor does it).
quality_bar: DTOs with class-validator consistent with existing DTOs; reuse the @CurrentUser decorator for REST.
Register ChatModule in V1Module; import AuthModule; register new schemas via MongooseModule.forFeature.
List new packages to install (@nestjs/websockets, @nestjs/platform-socket.io, socket.io) and why; add nothing unrelated.
Do not break existing modules. The User publicKey edit must be optional with default null.
work_order: 1. publicKey on User + keys endpoints + extract shared session-verification from JwtAuthGuard into a reusable AuthService method.
2. Conversation + Message schemas + Mongoose registration + ChatModule wired into V1Module.
3. REST: create/list conversations + cursor-paginated message history (with participant/GroupMember authorization).
4. Gateway: connection auth (shared verification) + presence + join-room + send-message/new-message.

postman_update.when: After the code is implemented and confirmed working.
steps: Locate the existing Postman collection in the repo (look for a *.postman_collection.json file and any environment file). If none exists, tell the user and propose creating one rather than guessing.

Match the existing collection's conventions: variables (e.g. {{baseUrl}}, {{token}}), folder organization, and how auth is set (collection-level vs per-request Bearer / x-auth-token). Do not change existing requests.

Add the new REST requests under a new 'Chat' folder (and the key endpoints under the existing 'Auth' folder): auth/keys, auth/public-key, chat/conversations, chat/conversations/list, chat/messages. For each, include the correct method, full {{baseUrl}} URL, JWT auth, and a realistic example body matching the DTOs. Use placeholder ciphertext strings (e.g. '<base64-ciphertext>') so requests are runnable in shape.

Document the Socket.IO events: if the collection format supports a Socket.IO request, add one pointing at the socket URL with auth.token and the events (join-room, send-message, typing, read-receipt) and their payloads. If not, add a folder description or markdown note listing each event, direction, and payload shape.
Save the updated collection file in place and summarize exactly what was added.

start_with: Step 1 (verify auth internals + report plan), then wait for user confirmation before implementing.
